payload.title: Replay protection is optional, allowing rollback to any previously valid signed root
payload.description: `minimumGeneration` is optional and replay validation runs only when callers provide it. A caller using the otherwise valid documented options can therefore accept an older, still correctly signed root after a newer generation has been activated. An attacker able to replay registry or transport responses could restore superseded authority, security, identity, or serving policies. Make an authoritative generation floor mandatory or obtain it from trusted persistent state inside the verifier; fail closed when that state is unavailable.

payload.title: Verifier accepts roots before their `valid_from` activation time
payload.description: Verification checks only that `valid_from` is not earlier than `issued_at`; it never compares either timestamp with a trusted current time. Consequently, a legitimately signed root scheduled for future activation is accepted immediately. Prematurely exposed or replayed future roots can activate authority or security-policy changes before their intended approval window. Require a trusted clock during verification and reject roots where `valid_from` is in the future beyond the permitted skew.
